subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Cosmic Microwave Background
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Cosmic Microwave Background.

hook.text: In 1965, two radio engineers found mysterious static in their antenna. It came from every direction equally.

buildup.text: Arno Penzias and Robert Wilson couldn't eliminate the noise. They even removed pigeon droppings from the dish.

discovery.text: The static was the Cosmic Microwave Background—the afterglow of the Big Bang, cooled to just 2.7 degrees above absolute zero.

twist.text: They won the 1978 Nobel Prize for a discovery they stumbled upon by accident while doing unrelated work.

climax.text: The CMB is the strongest evidence for the Big Bang. It matches predictions to extraordinary precision.

punchline.text: Pigeon poop couldn't explain it. It was the glow of creation.

quiz.question: How was the Cosmic Microwave Background discovered?
options: Accidentally, by two radio engineers who couldn't eliminate mysterious static
Deliberately, by a space telescope designed to detect it
Through mathematical predictions that were later confirmed
